user_message: I need the team view on security posture. This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-21 08:00 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 11:49 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-02-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:07:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:12:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:15:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:20:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
